  641 | m_rrData.recentPerformanceAdj,
  642 | m_rrData.optimalRiskReward,

    1 | ﻿//+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    2 | //|                                           Risk_KellyCriterion.mqh |
    3 | //|                        SONIC R MC - KELLY CRITERION POSITION SIZING |
    4 | //|                            🎯 MATHEMATICAL LOT CALCULATION        |
    5 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    6 | 
    7 | #ifndef RISK_KELLY_CRITERION_MQH
    8 | #define RISK_KELLY_CRITERION_MQH
    9 | 
   10 | #include "01_Core_12_SonicEnums.mqh"
   11 | 
   12 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   13 | //| Kelly Criterion Performance Tracking                             |
   14 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   15 | struct KellyPerformanceData {
   16 | double totalTrades;
   17 | double winningTrades;
   18 | double losingTrades;
   19 | double totalProfit;
   20 | double totalLoss;
   21 | double largestWin;
   22 | double largestLoss;
   23 | double avgWin;
   24 | double avgLoss;
   25 | double winRate;
   26 | double profitFactor;
   27 | double kellyFraction;
   28 | double edgeRatio;
   29 | datetime lastUpdate;
   30 | 
   31 | void Reset() {
   32 | totalTrades = 0;
   33 | winningTrades = 0;
   34 | losingTrades = 0;
   35 | totalProfit = 0;
   36 | totalLoss = 0;
   37 | largestWin = 0;
   38 | largestLoss = 0;
   39 | avgWin = 0;
   40 | avgLoss = 0;
   41 | winRate = 0;
   42 | profitFactor = 0;
   43 | kellyFraction = 0;
   44 | edgeRatio = 0;
   45 | lastUpdate = 0;
   46 | }
   47 | 
   48 | void Calculate() {
   49 | if(totalTrades > 0) {
   50 | winRate = winningTrades / totalTrades;
   51 | avgWin = (winningTrades > 0) ? totalProfit / winningTrades : 0;
   52 | avgLoss = (losingTrades > 0) ? totalLoss / losingTrades : 0;
   53 | profitFactor = (totalLoss > 0) ? totalProfit / totalLoss : 0;
   54 | 
   55 | // Kelly Fraction = (bp - q) / b
   56 | // where: b = odds received (avgWin/avgLoss), p = win probability, q = loss probability
   57 | if(avgLoss > 0) {
   58 | double b = avgWin / avgLoss;  // Odds ratio
   59 | double p = winRate;           // Win probability  
   60 | double q = 1 - p;            // Loss probability
   61 | kellyFraction = (b * p - q) / b;
   62 | edgeRatio = (p * avgWin) - (q * avgLoss);
   63 | }
   64 | }
   65 | }
   66 | };
   67 | 
   68 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   69 | //| 🎯 KELLY CRITERION POSITION SIZER - SONIC R IMPLEMENTATION      |
   70 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   71 | class CKellyCriterionSizer
   72 | {
   73 | private:
   74 | KellyPerformanceData    m_performance;
   75 | double                  m_safetyFactor;      // Reduce Kelly by this factor for safety
   76 | double                  m_maxRiskPercent;    // Maximum risk allowed per trade
   77 | double                  m_minRiskPercent;    // Minimum risk per trade
   78 | bool                    m_adaptiveMode;      // Use adaptive Kelly calculation
   79 | int                     m_minTradesRequired; // Minimum trades before using Kelly
   80 | 
   81 | // Performance tracking
   82 | double                  m_tradeHistory[200];  // Store last 200 trade results
   83 | int                     m_historyIndex;
   84 | int                     m_historyCount;
   85 | 
   86 | public:
   87 | CKellyCriterionSizer()
   88 | {
   89 | m_safetyFactor = 0.5;
   90 | m_maxRiskPercent = 2.0;
   91 | m_minRiskPercent = 0.2;
   92 | m_adaptiveMode = true;
   93 | m_minTradesRequired = 30;
   94 | m_historyIndex = 0;
   95 | m_historyCount = 0;
   96 | m_performance.Reset();
   97 | ArrayInitialize(m_tradeHistory, 0.0);
   98 | }
   99 | 
  100 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  101 | //| 🎯 MAIN KELLY POSITION SIZE CALCULATION                         |
  102 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  103 | double CalculateKellySize(double signalConfidence, double stopLossPoints, double takeProfitPoints, double accountBalance)
  104 | {
  105 | // Update performance metrics first
  106 | UpdatePerformanceMetrics();
  107 | 
  108 | // If insufficient trade history, use conservative sizing
  109 | if(m_performance.totalTrades < m_minTradesRequired) {
  110 | double conservativeSize = CalculateConservativeSize(signalConfidence, accountBalance);
  111 | Print(StringFormat("[🎯 KELLY] Insufficient history (%d trades), using conservative: %.3f%%", 
  112 | (int)m_performance.totalTrades, conservativeSize));
  113 | return conservativeSize;
  114 | }
  115 | 
  116 | // Calculate base Kelly fraction
  117 | double kellyFraction = CalculateKellyFraction(stopLossPoints, takeProfitPoints);
  118 | 
  119 | // Apply signal confidence adjustment
  120 | kellyFraction *= GetConfidenceAdjustment(signalConfidence);
  121 | 
  122 | // Apply safety factor (Boss's requirement for conservative approach)
  123 | kellyFraction *= m_safetyFactor;
  124 | 
  125 | // Apply market condition adjustment
  126 | kellyFraction *= GetMarketConditionAdjustment();
  127 | 
  128 | // Convert to risk percentage and apply limits
  129 | double riskPercent = MathMax(m_minRiskPercent, MathMin(m_maxRiskPercent, kellyFraction * 100));
  130 | 
  131 | Print(StringFormat("[🖖 KELLY FINAL] Raw: %.3f, Adjusted: %.3f, Final Risk: %.2f%% (Confidence: %.1f%%)", 
  132 | m_performance.kellyFraction, kellyFraction, riskPercent, signalConfidence * 100));
  133 | 
  134 | return riskPercent;
  135 | }
  136 | 
  137 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  138 | //| 🎯 CALCULATE KELLY FRACTION                                      |
  139 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  140 | double CalculateKellyFraction(double stopLossPoints, double takeProfitPoints)
  141 | {
  142 | if(stopLossPoints <= 0 || takeProfitPoints <= 0) return 0.01; // 1% fallback
  143 | 
  144 | // Calculate risk-reward ratio
  145 | double riskRewardRatio = takeProfitPoints / stopLossPoints;
  146 | 
  147 | // Kelly Fraction = (bp - q) / b
  148 | // where: b = risk-reward ratio, p = win probability, q = loss probability
  149 | double b = riskRewardRatio;
  150 | double p = m_performance.winRate;
  151 | double q = 1.0 - p;
  152 | 
  153 | if(b <= 0) return 0.01;
  154 | 
  155 | double kellyFraction = (b * p - q) / b;
  156 | 
  157 | // Kelly should be positive for profitable system
  158 | if(kellyFraction <= 0) {
  159 | Print("[⚠️ KELLY] Negative Kelly fraction detected: ", kellyFraction, " - System may not be profitable");
  160 | return 0.01; // Minimum size
  161 | }
  162 | 
  163 | return kellyFraction;
  164 | }
  165 | 
  166 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  167 | //| 🎯 SIGNAL CONFIDENCE ADJUSTMENT                                  |
  168 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  169 | double GetConfidenceAdjustment(double signalConfidence)
  170 | {
  171 | // Higher confidence = can use more of Kelly fraction
  172 | if(signalConfidence >= 0.90) return 1.2;      // Very high confidence: use 120% of Kelly
  173 | else if(signalConfidence >= 0.80) return 1.0; // High confidence: full Kelly
  174 | else if(signalConfidence >= 0.70) return 0.8; // Medium confidence: 80% of Kelly
  175 | else if(signalConfidence >= 0.60) return 0.6; // Lower confidence: 60% of Kelly
  176 | else return 0.4;                               // Low confidence: 40% of Kelly
  177 | }
  178 | 
  179 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  180 | //| 🎯 MARKET CONDITION ADJUSTMENT                                   |
  181 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  182 | double GetMarketConditionAdjustment()
  183 | {
  184 | double adjustment = 1.0;
  185 | 
  186 | // Volatility adjustment
  187 | double atr = GetCurrentATR();
  188 | double avgATR = GetAverageATR(20);
  189 | 
  190 | if(avgATR > 0) {
  191 | double volatilityRatio = atr / avgATR;
  192 | if(volatilityRatio > 1.5) {
  193 | adjustment *= 0.7;  // High volatility: reduce position size
  194 | Print("[🌪️ KELLY] High volatility adjustment: 0.7");
  195 | }
  196 | else if(volatilityRatio < 0.7) {
  197 | adjustment *= 1.1;  // Low volatility: can increase slightly
  198 | Print("[😴 KELLY] Low volatility adjustment: 1.1");
  199 | }
  200 | }
  201 | 
  202 | // Drawdown protection
  203 | double currentDD = CalculateCurrentDrawdown();
  204 | if(currentDD > 0.05) {  // If drawdown > 5%
  205 | adjustment *= (1.0 - currentDD);  // Reduce size proportionally
  206 | Print(StringFormat("[🛡️ KELLY] Drawdown protection: %.1f%% DD, adjustment: %.2f", currentDD * 100, adjustment));
  207 | }
  208 | 
  209 | return MathMax(0.3, adjustment); // Never reduce below 30%
  210 | }
  211 | 
  212 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  213 | //| 🎯 CONSERVATIVE SIZING FOR INSUFFICIENT HISTORY                 |
  214 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  215 | double CalculateConservativeSize(double signalConfidence, double accountBalance)
  216 | {
  217 | double baseSize = 1.0; // 1% base risk
  218 | 
  219 | // Adjust based on signal confidence
  220 | if(signalConfidence >= 0.85) baseSize = 1.5;
  221 | else if(signalConfidence >= 0.70) baseSize = 1.2;
  222 | else if(signalConfidence >= 0.60) baseSize = 1.0;
  223 | else baseSize = 0.5;
  224 | 
  225 | // Progressive increase as we gain more trades
  226 | double progressFactor = m_performance.totalTrades / m_minTradesRequired;
  227 | baseSize *= (0.5 + 0.5 * progressFactor); // Start at 50%, gradually increase
  228 | 
  229 | return MathMax(m_minRiskPercent, MathMin(m_maxRiskPercent * 0.5, baseSize));
  230 | }
  231 | 
  232 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  233 | //| 🎯 UPDATE PERFORMANCE METRICS                                    |
  234 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  235 | void UpdatePerformanceMetrics()
  236 | {
  237 | m_performance.Calculate();
  238 | m_performance.lastUpdate = TimeCurrent();
  239 | }
  240 | 
  241 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  242 | //| 🎯 ADD TRADE RESULT TO HISTORY                                   |
  243 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  244 | void AddTradeResult(double profit, double riskAmount)
  245 | {
  246 | // Calculate percentage return
  247 | double percentReturn = (riskAmount > 0) ? (profit / riskAmount) * 100 : 0;
  248 | 
  249 | // Add to history
  250 | m_tradeHistory[m_historyIndex] = percentReturn;
  251 | m_historyIndex = (m_historyIndex + 1) % 200;
  252 | if(m_historyCount < 200) m_historyCount++;
  253 | 
  254 | // Update performance data
  255 | m_performance.totalTrades++;
  256 | if(profit > 0) {
  257 | m_performance.winningTrades++;
  258 | m_performance.totalProfit += profit;
  259 | if(profit > m_performance.largestWin) m_performance.largestWin = profit;
  260 | } else if(profit < 0) {
  261 | m_performance.losingTrades++;
  262 | m_performance.totalLoss += MathAbs(profit);
  263 | if(MathAbs(profit) > m_performance.largestLoss) m_performance.largestLoss = MathAbs(profit);
  264 | }
  265 | 
  266 | UpdatePerformanceMetrics();
  267 | 
  268 | Print(StringFormat("[📊 KELLY] Trade added: P&L=%.2f, Total Trades=%d, Win Rate=%.1f%%, Kelly Fraction=%.3f",
  269 | profit, (int)m_performance.totalTrades, m_performance.winRate * 100, m_performance.kellyFraction));
  270 | }
  271 | 
  272 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  273 | //| 🎯 HELPER METHODS                                                |
  274 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  275 | double GetCurrentATR()
  276 | {
  277 | int atrHandle = iATR(_Symbol, PERIOD_H1, 14);
  278 | double atrBuffer[];
  279 | ArrayResize(atrBuffer, 1);
  280 | if(CopyBuffer(atrHandle, 0, 0, 1, atrBuffer) > 0) {
  281 | IndicatorRelease(atrHandle);
  282 | return atrBuffer[0];
  283 | }
  284 | IndicatorRelease(atrHandle);
  285 | return 0.0001; // Fallback
  286 | }
  287 | 
  288 | double GetAverageATR(int period)
  289 | {
  290 | int atrHandle = iATR(_Symbol, PERIOD_H1, 14);
  291 | double atrBuffer[];
  292 | ArrayResize(atrBuffer, 50);
  293 | ArraySetAsSeries(atrBuffer, true);
  294 | if(CopyBuffer(atrHandle, 0, 0, period, atrBuffer) >= period) {
  295 | double sum = 0;
  296 | for(int i = 0; i < period; i++) sum += atrBuffer[i];
  297 | IndicatorRelease(atrHandle);
  298 | return sum / (double)period;
  299 | }
  300 | IndicatorRelease(atrHandle);
  301 | return 0.0001; // Fallback
  302 | }
  303 | 
  304 | double CalculateCurrentDrawdown()
  305 | {
  306 | if(m_historyCount < 10) return 0.0;
  307 | 
  308 | double peak = 0;
  309 | double runningSum = 0;
  310 | double maxDD = 0;
  311 | 
  312 | for(int i = 0; i < m_historyCount; i++) {
  313 | runningSum += m_tradeHistory[i];
  314 | if(runningSum > peak) peak = runningSum;
  315 | double currentDD = (peak - runningSum) / 100.0; // Convert to percentage
  316 | if(currentDD > maxDD) maxDD = currentDD;
  317 | }
  318 | 
  319 | return maxDD;
  320 | }
  321 | 
  322 | double CalculateDynamicKelly(double winRate, double avgWin, double avgLoss, double volatility) {
  323 | double baseKelly = (avgWin * winRate - avgLoss * (1 - winRate)) / avgWin;
  324 | double adjustFactor = 1.0 - (volatility / 100.0);  // Reduce in high vol
  325 | return MathMin(baseKelly * adjustFactor, 0.25);
  326 | }
  327 | 
  328 | // Public getters
  329 | KellyPerformanceData GetPerformanceData() const { return m_performance; }
  330 | double GetCurrentKellyFraction() const { return m_performance.kellyFraction; }
  331 | double GetSafetyFactor() const { return m_safetyFactor; }
  332 | void SetSafetyFactor(double factor) { m_safetyFactor = MathMax(0.1, MathMin(1.0, factor)); }
  333 | 
  334 | string GetKellyReport()
  335 | {
  336 | return StringFormat(
  337 | "🎯 KELLY CRITERION REPORT\nTotal Trades: %d\nWin Rate: %.1f%%\nAvg Win: %.2f | Avg Loss: %.2f\nProfit Factor: %.2f\nKelly Fraction: %.3f\nSafety Factor: %.2f\nCurrent Risk Range: %.1f%% - %.1f%%",
  338 | (int)m_performance.totalTrades,
  339 | m_performance.winRate * 100,
  340 | m_performance.avgWin, m_performance.avgLoss,
  341 | m_performance.profitFactor,
  342 | m_performance.kellyFraction,
  343 | m_safetyFactor,
  344 | m_minRiskPercent, m_maxRiskPercent
  345 | );
  346 | }
  347 | };
  348 | 
  349 | #endif // RISK_KELLY_CRITERION_MQH 
  350 | 
  351 |

   18 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   19 | //| Market Cycle Analysis Data                                       |
   20 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   21 | struct MarketCycleData
   22 | {
   23 | ENUM_MARKET_CYCLE currentCycle;
   24 | ENUM_MARKET_CYCLE previousCycle;
   25 | double cycleConfidence;         // Confidence in cycle identification (0-1)
   26 | datetime cycleStartTime;        // When current cycle started
   27 | int cycleDuration;             // Duration in bars
   28 | double cycleStrength;          // Strength of current cycle (0-1)
   29 |

   36 |

   39 | bool tradingAllowed;           // Whether trading is recommended
   40 |

   42 | {

   44 | previousCycle = CYCLE_UNKNOWN;
   45 | cycleConfidence = 0.0;
   46 | cycleStartTime = 0;
   47 | cycleDuration = 0;
   48 | cycleStrength = 0.0;
   49 | accumulationScore = 0.0;
   50 | markupScore = 0.0;
   51 | distributionScore = 0.0;
   52 | markdownScore = 0.0;
   53 | transitionScore = 0.0;
   54 | riskMultiplier = 1.0;
   55 | tradingAllowed = true;
   56 | }
   57 | };
   58 | 
   59 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   60 | //| Volume Profile Analysis for Cycle Detection                     |
   61 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   62 | struct VolumeProfileData
   63 | {
   64 | double volumeDelta;            // Volume buying vs selling pressure
   65 | double priceRange;             // Current price range compared to average
   66 | double volumeSpike;            // Volume spike indicator
   67 | double absorptionLevel;        // Volume absorption at key levels
   68 | double distributionPattern;    // Distribution pattern strength
   69 | 
   70 | void Calculate()
   71 | {
   72 | // Calculate volume delta (simplified)
   73 | long currentVolume = iVolume(_Symbol, PERIOD_CURRENT, 0);
   74 | long prevVolume = iVolume(_Symbol, PERIOD_CURRENT, 1);
   75 | volumeDelta = (prevVolume > 0) ? (double)(currentVolume - prevVolume) / prevVolume : 0.0;
   76 | 
   77 | // Calculate price range
   78 | MqlRates rates[];
   79 | ArrayResize(rates, 20);
   80 | ArraySetAsSeries(rates, true);
   81 | if(CopyRates(_Symbol, PERIOD_CURRENT, 0, 20, rates) >= 20) {
   82 | double currentRange = rates[0].high - rates[0].low;
   83 | double avgRange = 0;
   84 | for(int i = 1; i < 20; i++) {
   85 | avgRange += (rates[i].high - rates[i].low);
   86 | }
   87 | avgRange /= 19.0;
   88 | priceRange = (avgRange > 0) ? currentRange / avgRange : 1.0;
   89 | }
   90 | 
   91 | // Volume spike detection
   92 | long avgVolume = 0;
   93 | for(int i = 1; i <= 10; i++) {
   94 | avgVolume += iVolume(_Symbol, PERIOD_CURRENT, i);
   95 | }
   96 | avgVolume /= 10;
   97 | volumeSpike = (avgVolume > 0) ? (double)currentVolume / avgVolume : 1.0;
   98 | }
   99 | };
  100 |

    1 | ﻿//+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    2 | //|                            Risk_RealTimePerformanceFeedback.mqh |
    3 | //|              SONIC R MC - REAL-TIME PERFORMANCE FEEDBACK LOOP    |
    4 | //|                   ðŸŽ¯ QUYáº¾T Äá»ŠNH Sá» 9: FEEDBACK BREAKTHROUGH      |
    5 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
    6 | 
    7 | #ifndef RISK_REAL_TIME_PERFORMANCE_FEEDBACK_MQH
    8 | #define RISK_REAL_TIME_PERFORMANCE_FEEDBACK_MQH
    9 | 
   10 | #include "01_Core_12_SonicEnums.mqh"
   11 | 
   12 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   13 | //| Performance Trend Analysis                                       |
   14 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   15 | enum ENUM_PERFORMANCE_TREND
   16 | {
   17 | TREND_STRONG_UP,        // Strong upward performance trend
   18 | TREND_MODERATE_UP,      // Moderate upward trend
   19 | TREND_FLAT,             // Flat/sideways performance
   20 | TREND_MODERATE_DOWN,    // Moderate downward trend
   21 | TREND_STRONG_DOWN,      // Strong downward trend
   22 | TREND_VOLATILE,         // Highly volatile, no clear trend
   23 | TREND_INSUFFICIENT      // Insufficient data for trend analysis
   24 | };
   25 | 
   26 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   27 | //| Real-Time Performance Metrics                                   |
   28 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   29 | struct RealTimePerformanceData
   30 | {
   31 | // Recent performance tracking (sliding windows)
   32 | double recentWinRate10;           // Win rate last 10 trades
   33 | double recentWinRate20;           // Win rate last 20 trades
   34 | double recentWinRate50;           // Win rate last 50 trades
   35 | 
   36 | double recentProfitFactor10;      // Profit factor last 10 trades
   37 | double recentProfitFactor20;      // Profit factor last 20 trades
   38 | double recentProfitFactor50;      // Profit factor last 50 trades
   39 | 
   40 | // Consecutive performance tracking
   41 | int consecutiveWins;              // Current winning streak
   42 | int consecutiveLosses;            // Current losing streak
   43 | double consecutiveWinProfit;      // Profit from current win streak
   44 | double consecutiveLossAmount;     // Loss from current loss streak
   45 | 
   46 | // Performance trend analysis
   47 | ENUM_PERFORMANCE_TREND performanceTrend;
   48 | double trendStrength;             // Strength of current trend (0-1)
   49 | double trendDuration;             // How long trend has persisted
   50 | double trendConfidence;           // Confidence in trend assessment
   51 | 
   52 | // Risk adjustment factors
   53 | double adaptiveRiskMultiplier;    // Overall risk adjustment
   54 | double winStreakAdjustment;       // Adjustment for winning streaks
   55 | double lossStreakAdjustment;      // Adjustment for losing streaks
   56 | double volatilityAdjustment;      // Adjustment for performance volatility
   57 | 
   58 | // Performance quality metrics
   59 | double performanceQuality;        // Overall performance quality score
   60 | double consistencyScore;          // How consistent the performance is
   61 | double stabilityScore;            // How stable the performance is
   62 | double recoveryScore;             // How well system recovers from losses
   63 | 
   64 | // Warning flags
   65 | bool performanceWarning;          // General performance warning
   66 | bool drawdownWarning;             // Drawdown warning
   67 | bool volatilityWarning;           // Performance volatility warning
   68 | bool streakWarning;               // Excessive streak warning
   69 | 
   70 | void Reset()
   71 | {
   72 | recentWinRate10 = 0.5;
   73 | recentWinRate20 = 0.5;
   74 | recentWinRate50 = 0.5;
   75 | recentProfitFactor10 = 1.0;
   76 | recentProfitFactor20 = 1.0;
   77 | recentProfitFactor50 = 1.0;
   78 | consecutiveWins = 0;
   79 | consecutiveLosses = 0;
   80 | consecutiveWinProfit = 0.0;
   81 | consecutiveLossAmount = 0.0;
   82 | performanceTrend = TREND_INSUFFICIENT;
   83 | trendStrength = 0.0;
   84 | trendDuration = 0.0;
   85 | trendConfidence = 0.0;
   86 | adaptiveRiskMultiplier = 1.0;
   87 | winStreakAdjustment = 1.0;
   88 | lossStreakAdjustment = 1.0;
   89 | volatilityAdjustment = 1.0;
   90 | performanceQuality = 0.5;
   91 | consistencyScore = 0.5;
   92 | stabilityScore = 0.5;
   93 | recoveryScore = 0.5;
   94 | performanceWarning = false;
   95 | drawdownWarning = false;
   96 | volatilityWarning = false;
   97 | streakWarning = false;
   98 | }
   99 | };
  100 | 
  101 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  102 | //| Trade Result for Feedback Analysis                              |
  103 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  104 | struct TradeResult
  105 | {
  106 | ulong ticket;
  107 | datetime openTime;
  108 | datetime closeTime;
  109 | double openPrice;
  110 | double closePrice;
  111 | double volume;
  112 | double profit;
  113 | double commission;
  114 | double swap;
  115 | bool isWin;
  116 | double riskAmount;
  117 | double riskRewardRatio;
  118 | double holdingTimeHours;
  119 | string signal;
  120 | double confidence;
  121 | };
  122 | 
  123 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  124 | //| ðŸŽ¯ REAL-TIME PERFORMANCE FEEDBACK LOOP SYSTEM                   |
  125 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  126 | class CRealTimePerformanceFeedback
  127 | {
  128 | private:
  129 | RealTimePerformanceData m_performanceData;
  130 | 
  131 | // Trade history for analysis
  132 | TradeResult m_tradeHistory[200];      // Store last 200 trades
  133 | int m_tradeIndex;
  134 | int m_tradeCount;
  135 | 
  136 | // Performance time series
  137 | double m_equityCurve[500];            // Equity progression
  138 | double m_dailyReturns[100];           // Daily returns
  139 | int m_equityIndex;
  140 | int m_equityCount;
  141 | int m_dailyIndex;
  142 | int m_dailyCount;
  143 | 
  144 | // Feedback parameters
  145 | double m_feedbackSensitivity;         // How aggressively to adjust
  146 | bool m_enableFeedbackLoop;            // Whether feedback is active
  147 | double m_minRiskMultiplier;           // Minimum risk multiplier
  148 | double m_maxRiskMultiplier;           // Maximum risk multiplier
  149 | 
  150 | // Performance thresholds
  151 | double m_goodWinRateThreshold;        // Threshold for good win rate
  152 | double m_badWinRateThreshold;         // Threshold for bad win rate
  153 | double m_goodProfitFactorThreshold;   // Threshold for good profit factor
  154 | double m_badProfitFactorThreshold;    // Threshold for bad profit factor
  155 | 
  156 | // Adaptive learning parameters
  157 | double m_learningRate;                // Rate of parameter adaptation
  158 | bool m_conservativeMode;              // Whether in conservative mode
  159 | datetime m_lastPerformanceCheck;      // Last performance analysis time
  160 | 
  161 | public:
  162 | CRealTimePerformanceFeedback() {
  163 | m_performanceData.Reset();
  164 | 
  165 | // Initialize trade history
  166 | for(int i = 0; i < 200; i++) {
  167 | m_tradeHistory[i].ticket = 0;
  168 | m_tradeHistory[i].profit = 0.0;
  169 | m_tradeHistory[i].isWin = false;
  170 | }
  171 | 
  172 | m_tradeIndex = 0;
  173 | m_tradeCount = 0;
  174 | 
  175 | // Initialize arrays
  176 | ArrayInitialize(m_equityCurve, 0.0);
  177 | ArrayInitialize(m_dailyReturns, 0.0);
  178 | 
  179 | m_equityIndex = 0;
  180 | m_equityCount = 0;
  181 | m_dailyIndex = 0;
  182 | m_dailyCount = 0;
  183 | 
  184 | // Initialize parameters
  185 | m_feedbackSensitivity = 1.0;
  186 | m_enableFeedbackLoop = true;
  187 | m_minRiskMultiplier = 0.3;
  188 | m_maxRiskMultiplier = 1.8;
  189 | 
  190 | // Initialize thresholds
  191 | m_goodWinRateThreshold = 0.7;
  192 | m_badWinRateThreshold = 0.5;
  193 | m_goodProfitFactorThreshold = 1.8;
  194 | m_badProfitFactorThreshold = 1.2;
  195 | 
  196 | // Initialize learning parameters
  197 | m_learningRate = 0.1;
  198 | m_conservativeMode = false;
  199 | m_lastPerformanceCheck = TimeCurrent();
  200 | 
  201 | ::Print("[FEEDBACK] Real-Time Performance Feedback Loop initialized");
  202 | ::Print("[CONFIGURATION] Sensitivity: ", m_feedbackSensitivity, 
  203 | " | Risk Range: ", m_minRiskMultiplier, "x - ", m_maxRiskMultiplier, "x");
  204 | };
  205 | ~CRealTimePerformanceFeedback() {}
  206 | 
  207 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  208 | //| ðŸŽ¯ MAIN PERFORMANCE FEEDBACK PROCESSING                        |
  209 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  210 | void ProcessPerformanceFeedback()
  211 | {
  212 | // Update performance metrics
  213 | UpdatePerformanceMetrics();
  214 | 
  215 | // Analyze performance trend
  216 | AnalyzePerformanceTrend();
  217 | 
  218 | // Calculate adaptive adjustments
  219 | CalculateAdaptiveAdjustments();
  220 | 
  221 | // Apply feedback corrections
  222 | ApplyFeedbackCorrections();
  223 | 
  224 | // Update warning system
  225 | UpdatePerformanceWarnings();
  226 | 
  227 | // Log performance feedback
  228 | LogPerformanceFeedback();
  229 | 
  230 | m_lastPerformanceCheck = TimeCurrent();
  231 | }
  232 | 
  233 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  234 | //| ðŸŽ¯ ADD TRADE RESULT FOR ANALYSIS                               |
  235 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  236 | void AddTradeResult(ulong ticket, double profit, double riskAmount, 
  237 | double confidence, string signal)
  238 | {
  239 | // Store trade result
  240 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].ticket = ticket;
  241 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].profit = profit;
  242 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].riskAmount = riskAmount;
  243 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].confidence = confidence;
  244 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].signal = signal;
  245 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].isWin = (profit > 0);
  246 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].closeTime = TimeCurrent();
  247 | 
  248 | // Calculate additional metrics
  249 | if(riskAmount > 0) {
  250 | m_tradeHistory[m_tradeIndex].riskRewardRatio = profit / riskAmount;
  251 | }
  252 | 
  253 | // Update index and count
  254 | m_tradeIndex = (m_tradeIndex + 1) % 200;
  255 | if(m_tradeCount < 200) m_tradeCount++;
  256 | 
  257 | // Update consecutive streaks
  258 | UpdateConsecutiveStreaks(profit > 0);
  259 | 
  260 | // Trigger immediate feedback processing for significant events
  261 | if(ShouldTriggerImmediateFeedback(profit)) {
  262 | ProcessPerformanceFeedback();
  263 | }
  264 | }
  265 | 
  266 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  267 | //| ðŸŽ¯ PERFORMANCE METRICS UPDATE                                  |
  268 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  269 | void UpdatePerformanceMetrics()
  270 | {
  271 | if(m_tradeCount < 5) {
  272 | m_performanceData.Reset();
  273 | return;
  274 | }
  275 | 
  276 | // Calculate win rates for different periods
  277 | m_performanceData.recentWinRate10 = CalculateWinRate(10);
  278 | m_performanceData.recentWinRate20 = CalculateWinRate(20);
  279 | m_performanceData.recentWinRate50 = CalculateWinRate(50);
  280 | 
  281 | // Calculate profit factors for different periods
  282 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor10 = CalculateProfitFactor(10);
  283 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor20 = CalculateProfitFactor(20);
  284 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor50 = CalculateProfitFactor(50);
  285 | 
  286 | // Calculate quality metrics
  287 | CalculatePerformanceQualityMetrics();
  288 | }
  289 | 
  290 | double CalculateWinRate(int period)
  291 | {
  292 | if(m_tradeCount == 0) return 0.5;
  293 | 
  294 | int trades = MathMin(period, m_tradeCount);
  295 | int wins = 0;
  296 | 
  297 | for(int i = 0; i < trades; i++) {
  298 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  299 | if(m_tradeHistory[idx].isWin) wins++;
  300 | }
  301 | 
  302 | return (double)wins / trades;
  303 | }
  304 | 
  305 | double CalculateProfitFactor(int period)
  306 | {
  307 | if(m_tradeCount == 0) return 1.0;
  308 | 
  309 | int trades = MathMin(period, m_tradeCount);
  310 | double totalProfit = 0.0;
  311 | double totalLoss = 0.0;
  312 | 
  313 | for(int i = 0; i < trades; i++) {
  314 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  315 | double profit = m_tradeHistory[idx].profit;
  316 | if(profit > 0) totalProfit += profit;
  317 | else totalLoss += MathAbs(profit);
  318 | }
  319 | 
  320 | return (totalLoss > 0) ? totalProfit / totalLoss : 
  321 | (totalProfit > 0) ? 10.0 : 1.0;
  322 | }
  323 | 
  324 | void CalculatePerformanceQualityMetrics()
  325 | {
  326 | // Performance Quality Score (0-1)
  327 | double winRateScore = (m_performanceData.recentWinRate20 - 0.4) / 0.4; // Normalize from 40%
  328 | double profitFactorScore = (m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor20 - 1.0) / 2.0; // Normalize from 1.0
  329 | 
  330 | winRateScore = MathMax(0.0, MathMin(1.0, winRateScore));
  331 | profitFactorScore = MathMax(0.0, MathMin(1.0, profitFactorScore));
  332 | 
  333 | m_performanceData.performanceQuality = (winRateScore + profitFactorScore) / 2.0;
  334 | 
  335 | // Consistency Score
  336 | m_performanceData.consistencyScore = CalculateConsistencyScore();
  337 | 
  338 | // Stability Score
  339 | m_performanceData.stabilityScore = CalculateStabilityScore();
  340 | 
  341 | // Recovery Score
  342 | m_performanceData.recoveryScore = CalculateRecoveryScore();
  343 | }
  344 | 
  345 | double CalculateConsistencyScore()
  346 | {
  347 | if(m_tradeCount < 10) return 0.5;
  348 | 
  349 | // Calculate variance in returns
  350 | double mean = 0.0;
  351 | int trades = MathMin(20, m_tradeCount);
  352 | 
  353 | for(int i = 0; i < trades; i++) {
  354 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  355 | mean += m_tradeHistory[idx].profit;
  356 | }
  357 | mean /= trades;
  358 | 
  359 | double variance = 0.0;
  360 | for(int i = 0; i < trades; i++) {
  361 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  362 | double diff = m_tradeHistory[idx].profit - mean;
  363 | variance += diff * diff;
  364 | }
  365 | variance /= trades;
  366 | 
  367 | // Lower variance = higher consistency
  368 | double consistency = 1.0 / (1.0 + variance * 0.0001); // Scale factor
  369 | return MathMax(0.0, MathMin(1.0, consistency));
  370 | }
  371 | 
  372 | double CalculateStabilityScore()
  373 | {
  374 | if(m_tradeCount < 10) return 0.5;
  375 | 
  376 | // Compare different period win rates for stability
  377 | double wr10 = m_performanceData.recentWinRate10;
  378 | double wr20 = m_performanceData.recentWinRate20;
  379 | double wr50 = m_performanceData.recentWinRate50;
  380 | 
  381 | double maxDiff = MathMax(MathAbs(wr10 - wr20), MathAbs(wr20 - wr50));
  382 | 
  383 | // Lower difference = higher stability
  384 | double stability = 1.0 - (maxDiff * 2.0); // Scale factor
  385 | return MathMax(0.0, MathMin(1.0, stability));
  386 | }
  387 | 
  388 | double CalculateRecoveryScore()
  389 | {
  390 | // Analyze how well the system recovers from losing streaks
  391 | if(m_tradeCount < 15) return 0.5;
  392 | 
  393 | int recoveryCount = 0;
  394 | int lossStreakCount = 0;
  395 | bool inLossStreak = false;
  396 | int currentStreakLength = 0;
  397 | 
  398 | for(int i = m_tradeCount - 1; i >= 0; i--) {
  399 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  400 | bool isWin = m_tradeHistory[idx].isWin;
  401 | 
  402 | if(!isWin) {
  403 | if(!inLossStreak) {
  404 | inLossStreak = true;
  405 | currentStreakLength = 1;
  406 | } else {
  407 | currentStreakLength++;
  408 | }
  409 | } else {
  410 | if(inLossStreak && currentStreakLength >= 2) {
  411 | // End of loss streak, check for recovery
  412 | lossStreakCount++;
  413 | // Check next few trades for recovery
  414 | bool recovered = false;
  415 | for(int j = i + 1; j < MathMin(i + 4, m_tradeCount); j++) {
  416 | int recoveryIdx = (m_tradeIndex - j - 1 + 200) % 200;
  417 | if(m_tradeHistory[recoveryIdx].isWin) {
  418 | recovered = true;
  419 | break;
  420 | }
  421 | }
  422 | if(recovered) recoveryCount++;
  423 | }
  424 | inLossStreak = false;
  425 | currentStreakLength = 0;
  426 | }
  427 | }
  428 | 
  429 | return (lossStreakCount > 0) ? (double)recoveryCount / lossStreakCount : 0.8;
  430 | }
  431 | 
  432 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  433 | //| ðŸŽ¯ PERFORMANCE TREND ANALYSIS                                  |
  434 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  435 | void AnalyzePerformanceTrend()
  436 | {
  437 | if(m_tradeCount < 10) {
  438 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_INSUFFICIENT;
  439 | return;
  440 | }
  441 | 
  442 | // Calculate recent vs older performance
  443 | double recentPerf = CalculateAverageReturn(10);  // Last 10 trades
  444 | double olderPerf = CalculateAverageReturn(20, 10); // Trades 11-20
  445 | 
  446 | double perfDiff = recentPerf - olderPerf;
  447 | double perfVolatility = CalculateReturnVolatility(20);
  448 | 
  449 | // Determine trend
  450 | if(perfDiff > perfVolatility * 0.5) {
  451 | if(perfDiff > perfVolatility) {
  452 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_STRONG_UP;
  453 | m_performanceData.trendStrength = 0.8;
  454 | } else {
  455 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_MODERATE_UP;
  456 | m_performanceData.trendStrength = 0.6;
  457 | }
  458 | } else if(perfDiff < -perfVolatility * 0.5) {
  459 | if(perfDiff < -perfVolatility) {
  460 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_STRONG_DOWN;
  461 | m_performanceData.trendStrength = 0.8;
  462 | } else {
  463 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_MODERATE_DOWN;
  464 | m_performanceData.trendStrength = 0.6;
  465 | }
  466 | } else if(perfVolatility > CalculateAverageVolatility() * 1.5) {
  467 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_VOLATILE;
  468 | m_performanceData.trendStrength = 0.4;
  469 | } else {
  470 | m_performanceData.performanceTrend = TREND_FLAT;
  471 | m_performanceData.trendStrength = 0.2;
  472 | }
  473 | 
  474 | // Calculate trend confidence
  475 | m_performanceData.trendConfidence = MathMin(1.0, m_performanceData.trendStrength * 
  476 | (m_tradeCount / 20.0));
  477 | }
  478 | 
  479 | double CalculateAverageReturn(int period, int offset = 0)
  480 | {
  481 | if(m_tradeCount <= offset) return 0.0;
  482 | 
  483 | int trades = MathMin(period, m_tradeCount - offset);
  484 | if(trades <= 0) return 0.0;
  485 | 
  486 | double sum = 0.0;
  487 | for(int i = offset; i < offset + trades; i++) {
  488 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  489 | sum += m_tradeHistory[idx].profit;
  490 | }
  491 | 
  492 | return sum / trades;
  493 | }
  494 | 
  495 | double CalculateReturnVolatility(int period)
  496 | {
  497 | if(m_tradeCount < period) return 1.0;
  498 | 
  499 | double mean = CalculateAverageReturn(period);
  500 | double variance = 0.0;
  501 | 
  502 | for(int i = 0; i < period; i++) {
  503 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - i - 1 + 200) % 200;
  504 | double diff = m_tradeHistory[idx].profit - mean;
  505 | variance += diff * diff;
  506 | }
  507 | 
  508 | return MathSqrt(variance / period);
  509 | }
  510 | 
  511 | double CalculateAverageVolatility()
  512 | {
  513 | // Calculate historical average volatility for comparison
  514 | return CalculateReturnVolatility(MathMin(50, m_tradeCount));
  515 | }
  516 | 
  517 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  518 | //| ðŸŽ¯ ADAPTIVE ADJUSTMENTS CALCULATION                            |
  519 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  520 | void CalculateAdaptiveAdjustments()
  521 | {
  522 | // Base adjustment starts at neutral
  523 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier = 1.0;
  524 | 
  525 | // Adjust based on performance trend
  526 | AdjustForPerformanceTrend();
  527 | 
  528 | // Adjust for consecutive streaks
  529 | AdjustForConsecutiveStreaks();
  530 | 
  531 | // Adjust for win rate performance
  532 | AdjustForWinRatePerformance();
  533 | 
  534 | // Adjust for profit factor performance
  535 | AdjustForProfitFactorPerformance();
  536 | 
  537 | // Adjust for performance volatility
  538 | AdjustForPerformanceVolatility();
  539 | 
  540 | // Apply safety bounds
  541 | ApplyAdjustmentBounds();
  542 | }
  543 | 
  544 | void AdjustForPerformanceTrend()
  545 | {
  546 | double adjustment = 1.0;
  547 | 
  548 | switch(m_performanceData.performanceTrend) {
  549 | case TREND_STRONG_UP:
  550 | // Gradually increase risk for strong uptrend
  551 | adjustment = 1.0 + (m_performanceData.trendStrength * 0.3);
  552 | break;
  553 | 
  554 | case TREND_MODERATE_UP:
  555 | // Slight increase for moderate uptrend
  556 | adjustment = 1.0 + (m_performanceData.trendStrength * 0.15);
  557 | break;
  558 | 
  559 | case TREND_FLAT:
  560 | // Neutral for flat performance
  561 | adjustment = 1.0;
  562 | break;
  563 | 
  564 | case TREND_MODERATE_DOWN:
  565 | // Reduce risk for downtrend
  566 | adjustment = 1.0 - (m_performanceData.trendStrength * 0.2);
  567 | break;
  568 | 
  569 | case TREND_STRONG_DOWN:
  570 | // Significantly reduce risk for strong downtrend
  571 | adjustment = 1.0 - (m_performanceData.trendStrength * 0.4);
  572 | break;
  573 | 
  574 | case TREND_VOLATILE:
  575 | // Reduce risk during high volatility
  576 | adjustment = 1.0 - (m_performanceData.trendStrength * 0.3);
  577 | break;
  578 | }
  579 | 
  580 | // Apply trend confidence weighting
  581 | adjustment = 1.0 + (adjustment - 1.0) * m_performanceData.trendConfidence;
  582 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier *= adjustment;
  583 | }
  584 | 
  585 | void AdjustForConsecutiveStreaks()
  586 | {
  587 | // Win streak adjustment
  588 | if(m_performanceData.consecutiveWins >= 5) {
  589 | m_performanceData.winStreakAdjustment = 1.0 + (m_performanceData.consecutiveWins - 4) * 0.05;
  590 | m_performanceData.winStreakAdjustment = MathMin(1.3, m_performanceData.winStreakAdjustment);
  591 | } else {
  592 | m_performanceData.winStreakAdjustment = 1.0;
  593 | }
  594 | 
  595 | // Loss streak adjustment
  596 | if(m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses >= 3) {
  597 | m_performanceData.lossStreakAdjustment = 1.0 - (m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses - 2) * 0.1;
  598 | m_performanceData.lossStreakAdjustment = MathMax(0.6, m_performanceData.lossStreakAdjustment);
  599 | } else {
  600 | m_performanceData.lossStreakAdjustment = 1.0;
  601 | }
  602 | 
  603 | // Apply the more conservative adjustment
  604 | double streakAdjustment = MathMin(m_performanceData.winStreakAdjustment,
  605 | m_performanceData.lossStreakAdjustment);
  606 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier *= streakAdjustment;
  607 | }
  608 | 
  609 | void AdjustForWinRatePerformance()
  610 | {
  611 | double winRate = m_performanceData.recentWinRate20;
  612 | double adjustment = 1.0;
  613 | 
  614 | if(winRate > m_goodWinRateThreshold) {
  615 | // Good win rate: gradually increase risk
  616 | adjustment = 1.0 + (winRate - m_goodWinRateThreshold) * 0.5;
  617 | } else if(winRate < m_badWinRateThreshold) {
  618 | // Poor win rate: reduce risk
  619 | adjustment = 1.0 - (m_badWinRateThreshold - winRate) * 1.0;
  620 | }
  621 | 
  622 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier *= adjustment;
  623 | }
  624 | 
  625 | void AdjustForProfitFactorPerformance()
  626 | {
  627 | double pf = m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor20;
  628 | double adjustment = 1.0;
  629 | 
  630 | if(pf > m_goodProfitFactorThreshold) {
  631 | // Good profit factor: increase risk
  632 | adjustment = 1.0 + (pf - m_goodProfitFactorThreshold) * 0.1;
  633 | } else if(pf < m_badProfitFactorThreshold) {
  634 | // Poor profit factor: reduce risk
  635 | adjustment = 1.0 - (m_badProfitFactorThreshold - pf) * 0.3;
  636 | }
  637 | 
  638 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier *= adjustment;
  639 | }
  640 | 
  641 | void AdjustForPerformanceVolatility()
  642 | {
  643 | // Reduce risk if performance is highly volatile
  644 | double consistency = m_performanceData.consistencyScore;
  645 | double stability = m_performanceData.stabilityScore;
  646 | 
  647 | double volatilityAdjustment = (consistency + stability) / 2.0;
  648 | volatilityAdjustment = 0.7 + (volatilityAdjustment * 0.3); // Scale to 0.7-1.0
  649 | 
  650 | m_performanceData.volatilityAdjustment = volatilityAdjustment;
  651 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier *= volatilityAdjustment;
  652 | }
  653 | 
  654 | void ApplyAdjustmentBounds()
  655 | {
  656 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier = MathMax(m_minRiskMultiplier,
  657 | MathMin(m_maxRiskMultiplier,
  658 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier));
  659 | }
  660 | 
  661 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  662 | //| ðŸŽ¯ FEEDBACK CORRECTIONS APPLICATION                            |
  663 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  664 | void ApplyFeedbackCorrections()
  665 | {
  666 | // This is where the system would actually adjust trading parameters
  667 | // based on the calculated feedback adjustments
  668 | 
  669 | // Examples of what could be adjusted:
  670 | // - Signal confidence thresholds
  671 | // - Risk per trade
  672 | // - Take profit / stop loss ratios
  673 | // - Trading frequency
  674 | 
  675 | if(m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier < 0.8) {
  676 | // Defensive mode: reduce activity
  677 | m_conservativeMode = true;
  678 | } else if(m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier > 1.2) {
  679 | // Aggressive mode: can increase activity
  680 | m_conservativeMode = false;
  681 | }
  682 | }
  683 | 
  684 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  685 | //| ðŸŽ¯ WARNING SYSTEM                                              |
  686 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  687 | void UpdatePerformanceWarnings()
  688 | {
  689 | // Reset warnings
  690 | m_performanceData.performanceWarning = false;
  691 | m_performanceData.drawdownWarning = false;
  692 | m_performanceData.volatilityWarning = false;
  693 | m_performanceData.streakWarning = false;
  694 | 
  695 | // Check performance warnings
  696 | if(m_performanceData.recentWinRate20 < 0.4 || 
  697 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor20 < 1.0) {
  698 | m_performanceData.performanceWarning = true;
  699 | ::Print("[PERFORMANCE] Poor recent performance detected");
  700 | }
  701 | 
  702 | // Check streak warnings
  703 | if(m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses >= 5) {
  704 | m_performanceData.streakWarning = true;
  705 | ::Print("[STREAK] Excessive losing streak: ", m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses);
  706 | }
  707 | 
  708 | // Check volatility warnings
  709 | if(m_performanceData.consistencyScore < 0.3) {
  710 | m_performanceData.volatilityWarning = true;
  711 | ::Print("[VOLATILITY] High performance volatility detected");
  712 | }
  713 | 
  714 | // Check drawdown (simplified)
  715 | if(m_performanceData.consecutiveLossAmount > 1000.0) { // Adjust threshold as needed
  716 | m_performanceData.drawdownWarning = true;
  717 | ::Print("[DRAWDOWN] Significant drawdown detected");
  718 | }
  719 | }
  720 | 
  721 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  722 | //| ðŸŽ¯ HELPER METHODS                                              |
  723 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  724 | void UpdateConsecutiveStreaks(bool isWin)
  725 | {
  726 | if(isWin) {
  727 | m_performanceData.consecutiveWins++;
  728 | m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses = 0;
  729 | m_performanceData.consecutiveLossAmount = 0.0;
  730 | 
  731 | // Update win profit
  732 | if(m_tradeCount > 0) {
  733 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - 1 + 200) % 200;
  734 | m_performanceData.consecutiveWinProfit += m_tradeHistory[idx].profit;
  735 | }
  736 | } else {
  737 | m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses++;
  738 | m_performanceData.consecutiveWins = 0;
  739 | m_performanceData.consecutiveWinProfit = 0.0;
  740 | 
  741 | // Update loss amount
  742 | if(m_tradeCount > 0) {
  743 | int idx = (m_tradeIndex - 1 + 200) % 200;
  744 | m_performanceData.consecutiveLossAmount += MathAbs(m_tradeHistory[idx].profit);
  745 | }
  746 | }
  747 | }
  748 | 
  749 | bool ShouldTriggerImmediateFeedback(double profit)
  750 | {
  751 | // Trigger immediate feedback for significant events
  752 | return (MathAbs(profit) > 500.0 ||  // Large profit/loss
  753 | m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses >= 3 ||  // Loss streak
  754 | m_performanceData.consecutiveWins >= 5);     // Win streak
  755 | }
  756 | 
  757 | void LogPerformanceFeedback()
  758 | {
  759 | static datetime lastLog = 0;
  760 | if(TimeCurrent() - lastLog < 3600) return; // Log hourly
  761 | 
  762 | ::Print(StringFormat("[FEEDBACK] Trend: %s | Quality: %.2f | Risk Mult: %.2fx | Conservative: %s",
  763 | PerformanceTrendToString(m_performanceData.performanceTrend),
  764 | m_performanceData.performanceQuality,
  765 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier,
  766 | m_conservativeMode ? "YES" : "NO"));
  767 | 
  768 | lastLog = TimeCurrent();
  769 | }
  770 | 
  771 | string PerformanceTrendToString(ENUM_PERFORMANCE_TREND trend)
  772 | {
  773 | switch(trend) {
  774 | case TREND_STRONG_UP: return "STRONG_UP";
  775 | case TREND_MODERATE_UP: return "MODERATE_UP";
  776 | case TREND_FLAT: return "FLAT";
  777 | case TREND_MODERATE_DOWN: return "MODERATE_DOWN";
  778 | case TREND_STRONG_DOWN: return "STRONG_DOWN";
  779 | case TREND_VOLATILE: return "VOLATILE";
  780 | default: return "UNKNOWN";
  781 | }
  782 | }
  783 | 
  784 | // Public interface methods
  785 | double GetAdaptiveRiskMultiplier() const { return m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier; }
  786 | RealTimePerformanceData GetPerformanceData() const { return m_performanceData; }
  787 | bool IsInConservativeMode() const { return m_conservativeMode; }
  788 | bool HasPerformanceWarning() const { return m_performanceData.performanceWarning; }
  789 | 
  790 | void SetFeedbackSensitivity(double sensitivity) 
  791 | { 
  792 | m_feedbackSensitivity = MathMax(0.1, MathMin(2.0, sensitivity)); 
  793 | }
  794 | 
  795 | void SetRiskMultiplierBounds(double minMult, double maxMult)
  796 | {
  797 | m_minRiskMultiplier = MathMax(0.2, minMult);
  798 | m_maxRiskMultiplier = MathMin(2.0, maxMult);
  799 | }
  800 | 
  801 | void EnableFeedbackLoop(bool enable) { m_enableFeedbackLoop = enable; }
  802 | 
  803 | string GetPerformanceFeedbackReport()
  804 | {
  805 | return StringFormat(
  806 | "REAL-TIME PERFORMANCE FEEDBACK\n" +
  807 | "Performance Trend: %s (Strength: %.1f%%, Confidence: %.1f%%)\n" +
  808 | "Win Rates - 10T: %.1f%% | 20T: %.1f%% | 50T: %.1f%%\n" +
  809 | "Profit Factors - 10T: %.2f | 20T: %.2f | 50T: %.2f\n" +
  810 | "Streaks - Wins: %d | Losses: %d\n" +
  811 | "Quality Scores - Performance: %.2f | Consistency: %.2f | Stability: %.2f | Recovery: %.2f\n" +
  812 | "Adaptive Risk Multiplier: %.2fx\n" +
  813 | "Conservative Mode: %s\n" +
  814 | "Warnings - Performance: %s | Drawdown: %s | Volatility: %s | Streak: %s",
  815 | PerformanceTrendToString(m_performanceData.performanceTrend),
  816 | m_performanceData.trendStrength * 100,
  817 | m_performanceData.trendConfidence * 100,
  818 | m_performanceData.recentWinRate10 * 100,
  819 | m_performanceData.recentWinRate20 * 100,
  820 | m_performanceData.recentWinRate50 * 100,
  821 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor10,
  822 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor20,
  823 | m_performanceData.recentProfitFactor50,
  824 | m_performanceData.consecutiveWins,
  825 | m_performanceData.consecutiveLosses,
  826 | m_performanceData.performanceQuality,
  827 | m_performanceData.consistencyScore,
  828 | m_performanceData.stabilityScore,
  829 | m_performanceData.recoveryScore,
  830 | m_performanceData.adaptiveRiskMultiplier,
  831 | m_conservativeMode ? "YES" : "NO",
  832 | m_performanceData.performanceWarning ? "YES" : "NO",
  833 | m_performanceData.drawdownWarning ? "YES" : "NO",
  834 | m_performanceData.volatilityWarning ? "YES" : "NO",
  835 | m_performanceData.streakWarning ? "YES" : "NO"
  836 | );
  837 | }
  838 | };
  839 | 
  840 | 
  841 | #endif // RISK_REAL_TIME_PERFORMANCE_FEEDBACK_MQH

   12 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   13 | //| Calendar Effect Types                                            |
   14 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   15 | enum ENUM_CALENDAR_EFFECT
   16 | {
   17 | CALENDAR_MONTHLY_CYCLE,      // Monthly pattern effects
   18 | CALENDAR_QUARTERLY_CYCLE,    // Quarterly business cycles
   19 | CALENDAR_YEARLY_CYCLE,       // Annual seasonal patterns
   20 | CALENDAR_WEEK_IN_MONTH,      // Week position in month
   21 | CALENDAR_HOLIDAY_EFFECT,     // Holiday impact
   22 | CALENDAR_ECONOMIC_EVENTS,    // Economic calendar events
   23 | CALENDAR_OPTIONS_EXPIRY,     // Options expiry effects
   24 | CALENDAR_TAX_EFFECTS        // Tax season effects
   25 | };
   26 | 
   27 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   28 | //| Seasonality Analysis Data                                        |
   29 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
   30 | struct SeasonalityData
   31 | {
   32 | // Monthly effects (12 months)
   33 | double monthlyEffects[12];           // Effect strength per month
   34 | double monthlyVolatility[12];        // Volatility per month
   35 | double monthlyRiskAdjustment[12];    // Risk adjustment per month
   36 | 
   37 | // Weekly effects within month
   38 | double weeklyEffects[5];             // Effect for each week of month
   39 | double weeklyVolatility[5];          // Volatility per week
   40 | 
   41 | // Daily effects within month
   42 | double dailyEffects[31];             // Effect for each day of month
   43 | 
   44 | // Current assessments
   45 | double currentSeasonalEffect;        // Current seasonal effect
   46 | double currentRiskAdjustment;        // Current risk adjustment
   47 | ENUM_CALENDAR_EFFECT dominantEffect; // Most significant current effect
   48 | 
   49 | // Pattern recognition
   50 | bool isPositiveSeasonPeriod;         // Whether in positive season
   51 | bool isHighVolatilityPeriod;         // Whether in high volatility period
   52 | bool isTaxSeasonEffect;              // Tax season impact
   53 | bool isHolidayPeriod;               // Holiday period impact
   54 | 
   55 | void Initialize()
   56 | {
   57 | // Initialize monthly effects with historical patterns
   58 | // Based on general forex seasonal patterns
   59 | monthlyEffects[0] = 0.015;   // January - New Year momentum
   60 | monthlyEffects[1] = 0.012;   // February - Moderate
   61 | monthlyEffects[2] = 0.018;   // March - Quarter end
   62 | monthlyEffects[3] = 0.009;   // April - Tax season
   63 | monthlyEffects[4] = 0.005;   // May - "Sell in May"
   64 | monthlyEffects[5] = 0.003;   // June - Summer doldrums start
   65 | monthlyEffects[6] = 0.010;   // July - Mid-year adjustment
   66 | monthlyEffects[7] = 0.012;   // August - Summer recovery
   67 | monthlyEffects[8] = 0.008;   // September - Volatility return
   68 | monthlyEffects[9] = 0.015;   // October - Autumn momentum
   69 | monthlyEffects[10] = 0.017;  // November - Pre-holiday push
   70 | monthlyEffects[11] = 0.020;  // December - Year-end effects
   71 | 
   72 | // Initialize volatility patterns
   73 | for(int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
   74 | if(i >= 4 && i <= 6) {
   75 | monthlyVolatility[i] = 0.8; // Lower volatility in summer
   76 | } else if(i == 0 || i >= 9) {
   77 | monthlyVolatility[i] = 1.2; // Higher volatility in winter/autumn
   78 | } else {
   79 | monthlyVolatility[i] = 1.0; // Normal volatility
   80 | }
   81 | }
   82 | 
   83 | // Initialize weekly effects
   84 | for(int i = 0; i < 5; i++) {
   85 | weeklyEffects[i] = 0.0;
   86 | weeklyVolatility[i] = 1.0;
   87 | }
   88 | 
   89 | // Initialize daily effects
   90 | for(int i = 0; i < 31; i++) {
   91 | dailyEffects[i] = 0.0;
   92 | }
   93 | 
   94 | currentSeasonalEffect = 0.0;
   95 | currentRiskAdjustment = 1.0;
   96 | dominantEffect = CALENDAR_MONTHLY_CYCLE;
   97 | isPositiveSeasonPeriod = false;
   98 | isHighVolatilityPeriod = false;
   99 | isTaxSeasonEffect = false;
  100 | isHolidayPeriod = false;
  101 | }
  102 | 
  103 | void CalculateRiskAdjustments()
  104 | {
  105 | for(int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
  106 | if(monthlyEffects[i] > 0.015) {
  107 | monthlyRiskAdjustment[i] = 1.2; // Increase risk in good months
  108 | } else if(monthlyEffects[i] > 0.010) {
  109 | monthlyRiskAdjustment[i] = 1.0; // Normal risk
  110 | } else if(monthlyEffects[i] > 0.005) {
  111 | monthlyRiskAdjustment[i] = 0.9; // Slight reduction
  112 | } else {
  113 | monthlyRiskAdjustment[i] = 0.6; // Significant reduction in bad months
  114 | }
  115 | 
  116 | // Adjust for volatility
  117 | monthlyRiskAdjustment[i] *= (2.0 - monthlyVolatility[i]);
  118 | }
  119 | }
  120 | };
  121 | 
  122 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  123 | //| Holiday and Economic Event Calendar                             |
  124 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  125 | struct CalendarEventData
  126 | {
  127 | // Major holidays affecting markets
  128 | bool isNewYear;
  129 | bool isEasterWeek;
  130 | bool isThanksgivingWeek;
  131 | bool isChristmasWeek;
  132 | bool isNationalHoliday;
  133 | 
  134 | // Economic events
  135 | bool isNFPWeek;              // Non-Farm Payroll week
  136 | bool isFOMCWeek;             // Federal Reserve meeting
  137 | bool isECBWeek;              // European Central Bank meeting
  138 | bool isOptionsExpiryWeek;    // Major options expiry
  139 | bool isQuarterEnd;           // End of quarter
  140 | bool isYearEnd;              // End of year
  141 | 
  142 | // Tax periods
  143 | bool isTaxSeasonUS;          // US tax season (Mar-Apr)
  144 | bool isTaxSeasonEU;          // EU tax periods
  145 | bool isTaxSeasonJP;          // Japan tax period
  146 | 
  147 | // Market-specific events
  148 | bool isEarningsSeasonPeak;   // Peak earnings season
  149 | bool isDividendMonth;        // High dividend month
  150 | bool isRebalancingPeriod;    // Index rebalancing
  151 | 
  152 | void Reset()
  153 | {
  154 | isNewYear = false;
  155 | isEasterWeek = false;
  156 | isThanksgivingWeek = false;
  157 | isChristmasWeek = false;
  158 | isNationalHoliday = false;
  159 | isNFPWeek = false;
  160 | isFOMCWeek = false;
  161 | isECBWeek = false;
  162 | isOptionsExpiryWeek = false;
  163 | isQuarterEnd = false;
  164 | isYearEnd = false;
  165 | isTaxSeasonUS = false;
  166 | isTaxSeasonEU = false;
  167 | isTaxSeasonJP = false;
  168 | isEarningsSeasonPeak = false;
  169 | isDividendMonth = false;
  170 | isRebalancingPeriod = false;
  171 | }
  172 | };
  173 | 
  174 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  175 | //| ðŸŽ¯ SEASONALITY & CALENDAR EFFECTS MANAGER                       |
  176 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  177 | class CSeasonalityCalendarManager
  178 | {
  179 | private:
  180 | SeasonalityData m_seasonalData;
  181 | CalendarEventData m_calendarEvents;
  182 | 
  183 | // Historical performance tracking by time period
  184 | double m_monthlyPerformance[12][3];      // [month][year_offset] - 3 years of data
  185 | double m_weeklyPerformance[53][2];       // [week][year_offset] - 2 years of data
  186 | double m_dailyPerformance[366];          // Day of year performance
  187 | 
  188 | // Current time analysis
  189 | int m_currentMonth;
  190 | int m_currentWeek;
  191 | int m_currentDay;
  192 | int m_currentDayOfYear;
  193 | int m_currentQuarter;
  194 | int m_weekInMonth;
  195 | double m_dayInMonthPercent;
  196 | 
  197 | // Seasonality parameters
  198 | bool m_enableSeasonalAdjustment;
  199 | double m_seasonalSensitivity;            // How much to adjust for seasonality
  200 | bool m_enableCalendarEffects;
  201 | double m_calendarSensitivity;            // How much to adjust for calendar events
  202 | 
  203 | public:
  204 | CSeasonalityCalendarManager() {
  205 | m_seasonalData.Initialize();
  206 | m_seasonalData.CalculateRiskAdjustments();
  207 | m_calendarEvents.Reset();
  208 | 
  209 | // Initialize performance tracking arrays
  210 | for(int i = 0; i < 12; i++) {
  211 | for(int j = 0; j < 3; j++) {
  212 | m_monthlyPerformance[i][j] = 0.0;
  213 | }
  214 | }
  215 | 
  216 | for(int i = 0; i < 53; i++) {
  217 | for(int j = 0; j < 2; j++) {
  218 | m_weeklyPerformance[i][j] = 0.0;
  219 | }
  220 | }
  221 | 
  222 | for(int i = 0; i < 366; i++) {
  223 | m_dailyPerformance[i] = 0.0;
  224 | }
  225 | 
  226 | // Initialize time variables
  227 | m_currentMonth = 0;
  228 | m_currentWeek = 0;
  229 | m_currentDay = 0;
  230 | m_currentDayOfYear = 0;
  231 | m_currentQuarter = 0;
  232 | m_weekInMonth = 0;
  233 | m_dayInMonthPercent = 0.0;
  234 | 
  235 | // Initialize parameters
  236 | m_enableSeasonalAdjustment = true;
  237 | m_seasonalSensitivity = 1.0;
  238 | m_enableCalendarEffects = true;
  239 | m_calendarSensitivity = 1.0;
  240 | 
  241 | ::Print("[SEASONALITY] Seasonality & Calendar Effects Manager initialized");
  242 | ::Print("[CONFIGURATION] Seasonal Sensitivity: ", m_seasonalSensitivity, " | Calendar Effects: ", m_enableCalendarEffects ? "ON" : "OFF");
  243 | };
  244 | ~CSeasonalityCalendarManager() {}
  245 | 
  246 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  247 | //| ðŸŽ¯ MAIN SEASONALITY ADJUSTMENT CALCULATION                     |
  248 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  249 | double CalculateSeasonalityAdjustment()
  250 | {
  251 | // Update current time data
  252 | UpdateCurrentTimeData();
  253 | 
  254 | // Analyze calendar events
  255 | AnalyzeCalendarEvents();
  256 | 
  257 | // Calculate seasonal effects
  258 | CalculateSeasonalEffects();
  259 | 
  260 | // Calculate calendar event effects
  261 | CalculateCalendarEventEffects();
  262 | 
  263 | // Combine all effects
  264 | CombineSeasonalAndCalendarEffects();
  265 | 
  266 | // Update risk adjustment
  267 | UpdateRiskAdjustment();
  268 | 
  269 | // Log seasonal analysis
  270 | LogSeasonalAnalysis();
  271 | 
  272 | return m_seasonalData.currentRiskAdjustment;
  273 | }
  274 | 
  275 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  276 | //| ðŸŽ¯ CURRENT TIME DATA UPDATE                                    |
  277 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  278 | void UpdateCurrentTimeData()
  279 | {
  280 | MqlDateTime timeStruct;
  281 | TimeToStruct(TimeCurrent(), timeStruct);
  282 | 
  283 | m_currentMonth = timeStruct.mon - 1;        // 0-11 for array indexing
  284 | m_currentDay = timeStruct.day;
  285 | m_currentDayOfYear = timeStruct.day_of_year;
  286 | 
  287 | // Calculate week of year
  288 | datetime yearStart = StringToTime(IntegerToString(timeStruct.year) + ".01.01 00:00");
  289 | m_currentWeek = (int)((TimeCurrent() - yearStart) / (7 * 24 * 3600));
  290 | 
  291 | // Calculate quarter
  292 | m_currentQuarter = (m_currentMonth / 3) + 1;
  293 | 
  294 | // Calculate week within month
  295 | m_weekInMonth = ((m_currentDay - 1) / 7) + 1;
  296 | 
  297 | // Calculate day position within month
  298 | int daysInMonth = GetDaysInMonth(timeStruct.mon, timeStruct.year);
  299 | m_dayInMonthPercent = (double)m_currentDay / daysInMonth;
  300 | }
  301 | 
  302 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  303 | //| ðŸŽ¯ CALENDAR EVENTS ANALYSIS                                    |
  304 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  305 | void AnalyzeCalendarEvents()
  306 | {
  307 | m_calendarEvents.Reset();
  308 | 
  309 | MqlDateTime timeStruct;
  310 | TimeToStruct(TimeCurrent(), timeStruct);
  311 | 
  312 | // Check for major holidays
  313 | CheckMajorHolidays(timeStruct);
  314 | 
  315 | // Check for economic events
  316 | CheckEconomicEvents(timeStruct);
  317 | 
  318 | // Check for tax periods
  319 | CheckTaxPeriods(timeStruct);
  320 | 
  321 | // Check for market-specific events
  322 | CheckMarketEvents(timeStruct);
  323 | }
  324 | 
  325 | void CheckMajorHolidays(const MqlDateTime& timeStruct)
  326 | {
  327 | // New Year period (late December to early January)
  328 | if((timeStruct.mon == 12 && timeStruct.day >= 25) || 
  329 | (timeStruct.mon == 1 && timeStruct.day <= 7)) {
  330 | m_calendarEvents.isNewYear = true;
  331 | }
  332 | 
  333 | // Christmas week
  334 | if(timeStruct.mon == 12 && timeStruct.day >= 20) {
  335 | m_calendarEvents.isChristmasWeek = true;
  336 | }
  337 | 
  338 | // Thanksgiving week (4th Thursday of November in US)
  339 | if(timeStruct.mon == 11 && timeStruct.day >= 22 && timeStruct.day <= 28) {
  340 | m_calendarEvents.isThanksgivingWeek = true;
  341 | }
  342 | 
  343 | // Easter week (simplified - typically March/April)
  344 | if((timeStruct.mon == 3 || timeStruct.mon == 4) && 
  345 | (timeStruct.day_of_week >= 0 && timeStruct.day_of_week <= 1)) {
  346 | m_calendarEvents.isEasterWeek = true;
  347 | }
  348 | }
  349 | 
  350 | void CheckEconomicEvents(const MqlDateTime& timeStruct)
  351 | {
  352 | // Non-Farm Payroll (first Friday of month)
  353 | if(timeStruct.day <= 7 && timeStruct.day_of_week == 5) {
  354 | m_calendarEvents.isNFPWeek = true;
  355 | }
  356 | 
  357 | // FOMC meetings (approximately 8 times per year, specific dates)
  358 | // Simplified check for typical FOMC months
  359 | if((timeStruct.mon == 1 || timeStruct.mon == 3 || timeStruct.mon == 5 || 
  360 | timeStruct.mon == 6 || timeStruct.mon == 9 || timeStruct.mon == 11 || timeStruct.mon == 12) &&
  361 | (timeStruct.day >= 15 && timeStruct.day <= 25)) {
  362 | m_calendarEvents.isFOMCWeek = true;
  363 | }
  364 | 
  365 | // Options expiry (third Friday of month)
  366 | if(timeStruct.day >= 15 && timeStruct.day <= 21 && timeStruct.day_of_week == 5) {
  367 | m_calendarEvents.isOptionsExpiryWeek = true;
  368 | }
  369 | 
  370 | // Quarter end
  371 | if((timeStruct.mon == 3 || timeStruct.mon == 6 || timeStruct.mon == 9 || timeStruct.mon == 12) &&
  372 | timeStruct.day >= 25) {
  373 | m_calendarEvents.isQuarterEnd = true;
  374 | }
  375 | 
  376 | // Year end
  377 | if(timeStruct.mon == 12 && timeStruct.day >= 15) {
  378 | m_calendarEvents.isYearEnd = true;
  379 | }
  380 | }
  381 | 
  382 | void CheckTaxPeriods(const MqlDateTime& timeStruct)
  383 | {
  384 | // US tax season (March-April)
  385 | if(timeStruct.mon == 3 || timeStruct.mon == 4) {
  386 | m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonUS = true;
  387 | }
  388 | 
  389 | // EU tax periods (varies by country, but often Q1)
  390 | if(timeStruct.mon >= 1 && timeStruct.mon <= 3) {
  391 | m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonEU = true;
  392 | }
  393 | 
  394 | // Japan tax year end (March)
  395 | if(timeStruct.mon == 3) {
  396 | m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonJP = true;
  397 | }
  398 | }
  399 | 
  400 | void CheckMarketEvents(const MqlDateTime& timeStruct)
  401 | {
  402 | // Earnings season peak (typically January, April, July, October)
  403 | if(timeStruct.mon == 1 || timeStruct.mon == 4 || timeStruct.mon == 7 || timeStruct.mon == 10) {
  404 | if(timeStruct.day >= 15 && timeStruct.day <= 31) {
  405 | m_calendarEvents.isEarningsSeasonPeak = true;
  406 | }
  407 | }
  408 | 
  409 | // High dividend months (typically March, June, September, December)
  410 | if(timeStruct.mon == 3 || timeStruct.mon == 6 || timeStruct.mon == 9 || timeStruct.mon == 12) {
  411 | m_calendarEvents.isDividendMonth = true;
  412 | }
  413 | 
  414 | // Index rebalancing (typically quarter ends)
  415 | if(m_calendarEvents.isQuarterEnd) {
  416 | m_calendarEvents.isRebalancingPeriod = true;
  417 | }
  418 | }
  419 | 
  420 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  421 | //| ðŸŽ¯ SEASONAL EFFECTS CALCULATION                                |
  422 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  423 | void CalculateSeasonalEffects()
  424 | {
  425 | // Monthly effect
  426 | double monthlyEffect = m_seasonalData.monthlyEffects[m_currentMonth];
  427 | 
  428 | // Weekly effect within month
  429 | double weeklyEffect = 0.0;
  430 | if(m_weekInMonth >= 1 && m_weekInMonth <= 5) {
  431 | weeklyEffect = m_seasonalData.weeklyEffects[m_weekInMonth - 1];
  432 | }
  433 | 
  434 | // Day of month effect
  435 | double dailyEffect = CalculateDayOfMonthEffect();
  436 | 
  437 | // Combine seasonal effects
  438 | m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect = monthlyEffect + weeklyEffect + dailyEffect;
  439 | 
  440 | // Determine seasonal characteristics
  441 | m_seasonalData.isPositiveSeasonPeriod = (monthlyEffect > 0.010);
  442 | m_seasonalData.isHighVolatilityPeriod = (m_seasonalData.monthlyVolatility[m_currentMonth] > 1.1);
  443 | }
  444 | 
  445 | double CalculateDayOfMonthEffect()
  446 | {
  447 | // Day of month patterns
  448 | if(m_dayInMonthPercent < 0.2) {
  449 | return -0.005; // Beginning of month - typically bearish (bill paying)
  450 | } else if(m_dayInMonthPercent < 0.4) {
  451 | return 0.002;  // Early-mid month
  452 | } else if(m_dayInMonthPercent < 0.6) {
  453 | return 0.008;  // Mid month - typically bullish (salary payments)
  454 | } else if(m_dayInMonthPercent < 0.8) {
  455 | return 0.003;  // Late-mid month
  456 | } else {
  457 | return -0.007; // End of month - typically bearish (portfolio rebalancing)
  458 | }
  459 | }
  460 | 
  461 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  462 | //| ðŸŽ¯ CALENDAR EVENT EFFECTS CALCULATION                          |
  463 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  464 | void CalculateCalendarEventEffects()
  465 | {
  466 | double calendarEffect = 0.0;
  467 | 
  468 | // Holiday effects (generally reduce volatility and activity)
  469 | if(m_calendarEvents.isNewYear) calendarEffect -= 0.015;
  470 | if(m_calendarEvents.isChristmasWeek) calendarEffect -= 0.020;
  471 | if(m_calendarEvents.isThanksgivingWeek) calendarEffect -= 0.010;
  472 | if(m_calendarEvents.isEasterWeek) calendarEffect -= 0.008;
  473 | 
  474 | // Economic event effects (generally increase volatility)
  475 | if(m_calendarEvents.isNFPWeek) calendarEffect += 0.012;
  476 | if(m_calendarEvents.isFOMCWeek) calendarEffect += 0.015;
  477 | if(m_calendarEvents.isECBWeek) calendarEffect += 0.010;
  478 | if(m_calendarEvents.isOptionsExpiryWeek) calendarEffect += 0.008;
  479 | 
  480 | // Period-end effects
  481 | if(m_calendarEvents.isQuarterEnd) calendarEffect += 0.010;
  482 | if(m_calendarEvents.isYearEnd) calendarEffect += 0.015;
  483 | 
  484 | // Tax effects (vary by region)
  485 | if(m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonUS) calendarEffect -= 0.005;
  486 | if(m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonJP) calendarEffect -= 0.008;
  487 | 
  488 | // Market event effects
  489 | if(m_calendarEvents.isEarningsSeasonPeak) calendarEffect += 0.005;
  490 | if(m_calendarEvents.isRebalancingPeriod) calendarEffect += 0.007;
  491 | 
  492 | // Update calendar flags
  493 | m_seasonalData.isHolidayPeriod = (m_calendarEvents.isNewYear || m_calendarEvents.isChristmasWeek || 
  494 | m_calendarEvents.isThanksgivingWeek || m_calendarEvents.isEasterWeek);
  495 | m_seasonalData.isTaxSeasonEffect = (m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonUS || m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonEU || 
  496 | m_calendarEvents.isTaxSeasonJP);
  497 | 
  498 | // Store calendar effect (could be used for further analysis)
  499 | // m_calendarEventEffect = calendarEffect; // If we had this member variable
  500 | }
  501 | 
  502 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  503 | //| ðŸŽ¯ COMBINE EFFECTS AND CALCULATE RISK ADJUSTMENT               |
  504 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  505 | void CombineSeasonalAndCalendarEffects()
  506 | {
  507 | // Base seasonal effect
  508 | double totalEffect = m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect;
  509 | 
  510 | // Apply calendar event modifications
  511 | // (This is simplified - in reality you'd want more sophisticated combining)
  512 | 
  513 | // Adjust for dominant effect type
  514 | if(m_seasonalData.isHolidayPeriod) {
  515 | m_seasonalData.dominantEffect = CALENDAR_HOLIDAY_EFFECT;
  516 | } else if(m_seasonalData.isTaxSeasonEffect) {
  517 | m_seasonalData.dominantEffect = CALENDAR_TAX_EFFECTS;
  518 | } else if(m_calendarEvents.isQuarterEnd) {
  519 | m_seasonalData.dominantEffect = CALENDAR_QUARTERLY_CYCLE;
  520 | } else if(m_calendarEvents.isNFPWeek || m_calendarEvents.isFOMCWeek) {
  521 | m_seasonalData.dominantEffect = CALENDAR_ECONOMIC_EVENTS;
  522 | } else {
  523 | m_seasonalData.dominantEffect = CALENDAR_MONTHLY_CYCLE;
  524 | }
  525 | 
  526 | // Update total seasonal effect
  527 | m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect = totalEffect;
  528 | }
  529 | 
  530 | void UpdateRiskAdjustment()
  531 | {
  532 | // Start with monthly base adjustment
  533 | double adjustment = m_seasonalData.monthlyRiskAdjustment[m_currentMonth];
  534 | 
  535 | // Apply seasonal effect modifications
  536 | if(m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect > 0.015) {
  537 | adjustment *= 1.2; // Increase risk in very positive periods
  538 | } else if(m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect > 0.010) {
  539 | adjustment *= 1.0; // Normal risk in good periods
  540 | } else if(m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect > 0.005) {
  541 | adjustment *= 0.9; // Slight reduction in average periods
  542 | } else if(m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect > 0.000) {
  543 | adjustment *= 0.8; // Reduce risk in weak periods
  544 | } else {
  545 | adjustment *= 0.6; // Significantly reduce in negative periods
  546 | }
  547 | 
  548 | // Apply calendar event adjustments
  549 | if(m_seasonalData.isHolidayPeriod) {
  550 | adjustment *= 0.7; // Reduce risk during holidays
  551 | }
  552 | 
  553 | if(m_seasonalData.isTaxSeasonEffect) {
  554 | adjustment *= 0.8; // Reduce risk during tax seasons
  555 | }
  556 | 
  557 | if(m_calendarEvents.isNFPWeek || m_calendarEvents.isFOMCWeek) {
  558 | adjustment *= 0.9; // Slightly reduce risk during major events
  559 | }
  560 | 
  561 | if(m_calendarEvents.isYearEnd) {
  562 | adjustment *= 1.1; // Slightly increase for year-end momentum
  563 | }
  564 | 
  565 | // Apply sensitivity factor
  566 | double neutralAdjustment = 1.0;
  567 | adjustment = neutralAdjustment + (adjustment - neutralAdjustment) * m_seasonalSensitivity;
  568 | 
  569 | // Ensure reasonable bounds
  570 | m_seasonalData.currentRiskAdjustment = MathMax(0.4, MathMin(1.5, adjustment));
  571 | }
  572 | 
  573 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  574 | //| ðŸŽ¯ HELPER METHODS                                              |
  575 | //+------------------------------------------------------------------+
  576 | int GetDaysInMonth(int month, int year)
  577 | {
  578 | int daysInMonth[] = {31, 28, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31, 31, 30, 31, 30, 31};
  579 | 
  580 | // Check for leap year in February
  581 | if(month == 2) {
  582 | if((year % 4 == 0 && year % 100 != 0) || (year % 400 == 0)) {
  583 | return 29; // Leap year
  584 | }
  585 | }
  586 | 
  587 | return daysInMonth[month - 1];
  588 | }
  589 | 
  590 | void LogSeasonalAnalysis()
  591 | {
  592 | static datetime lastLog = 0;
  593 | if(TimeCurrent() - lastLog < 86400) return; // Log once per day
  594 | 
  595 | ::Print(StringFormat("[SEASONALITY] Month: %d | Effect: %.3f | Adjustment: %.2fx | Dominant: %s",
  596 | m_currentMonth + 1,
  597 | m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect,
  598 | m_seasonalData.currentRiskAdjustment,
  599 | CalendarEffectToString(m_seasonalData.dominantEffect)));
  600 | 
  601 | if(m_seasonalData.isHolidayPeriod) {
  602 | ::Print("[SEASONALITY] Holiday period detected - reducing risk");
  603 | }
  604 | 
  605 | if(m_seasonalData.isTaxSeasonEffect) {
  606 | ::Print("[SEASONALITY] Tax season effect active - adjusting risk");
  607 | }
  608 | 
  609 | lastLog = TimeCurrent();
  610 | }
  611 | 
  612 | string CalendarEffectToString(ENUM_CALENDAR_EFFECT effect)
  613 | {
  614 | switch(effect) {
  615 | case CALENDAR_MONTHLY_CYCLE: return "MONTHLY";
  616 | case CALENDAR_QUARTERLY_CYCLE: return "QUARTERLY";
  617 | case CALENDAR_YEARLY_CYCLE: return "YEARLY";
  618 | case CALENDAR_WEEK_IN_MONTH: return "WEEKLY";
  619 | case CALENDAR_HOLIDAY_EFFECT: return "HOLIDAY";
  620 | case CALENDAR_ECONOMIC_EVENTS: return "ECONOMIC";
  621 | case CALENDAR_OPTIONS_EXPIRY: return "OPTIONS";
  622 | case CALENDAR_TAX_EFFECTS: return "TAX";
  623 | default: return "UNKNOWN";
  624 | }
  625 | }
  626 | 
  627 | // Public interface methods
  628 | double GetSeasonalRiskAdjustment() const { return m_seasonalData.currentRiskAdjustment; }
  629 | SeasonalityData GetSeasonalData() const { return m_seasonalData; }
  630 | CalendarEventData GetCalendarEvents() const { return m_calendarEvents; }
  631 | bool IsHolidayPeriod() const { return m_seasonalData.isHolidayPeriod; }
  632 | bool IsPositiveSeasonPeriod() const { return m_seasonalData.isPositiveSeasonPeriod; }
  633 | 
  634 | void SetSeasonalSensitivity(double sensitivity) 
  635 | { 
  636 | m_seasonalSensitivity = MathMax(0.0, MathMin(2.0, sensitivity)); 
  637 | }
  638 | 
  639 | void EnableSeasonalAdjustment(bool enable) { m_enableSeasonalAdjustment = enable; }
  640 | void EnableCalendarEffects(bool enable) { m_enableCalendarEffects = enable; }
  641 | 
  642 | string GetSeasonalityReport()
  643 | {
  644 | return StringFormat(
  645 | "SEASONALITY & CALENDAR EFFECTS\n" +
  646 | "Current Month: %d (%s)\n" +
  647 | "Seasonal Effect: %.3f\n" +
  648 | "Risk Adjustment: %.2fx\n" +
  649 | "Dominant Effect: %s\n" +
  650 | "Holiday Period: %s\n" +
  651 | "Tax Season: %s\n" +
  652 | "Positive Season: %s\n" +
  653 | "High Volatility Period: %s\n" +
  654 | "Day in Month: %.1f%% | Week in Month: %d",
  655 | m_currentMonth + 1,
  656 | GetMonthName(m_currentMonth + 1),
  657 | m_seasonalData.currentSeasonalEffect,
  658 | m_seasonalData.currentRiskAdjustment,
  659 | CalendarEffectToString(m_seasonalData.dominantEffect),
  660 | m_seasonalData.isHolidayPeriod ? "YES" : "NO",
  661 | m_seasonalData.isTaxSeasonEffect ? "YES" : "NO",
  662 | m_seasonalData.isPositiveSeasonPeriod ? "YES" : "NO",
  663 | m_seasonalData.isHighVolatilityPeriod ? "YES" : "NO",
  664 | m_dayInMonthPercent * 100,
  665 | m_weekInMonth
  666 | );
  667 | }
  668 | 
  669 | string GetMonthName(int month)
  670 | {
  671 | string months[] = {"Jan", "Feb", "Mar", "Apr", "May", "Jun", 
  672 | "Jul", "Aug", "Sep", "Oct", "Nov", "Dec"};
  673 | if(month >= 1 && month <= 12) return months[month - 1];
  674 | return "Unknown";
  675 | }
  676 | };
  677 |
